Appearance
From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

The S&P 500 is a stock market index maintained by S&P Dow Jones Indices. It comprises 503 common stocks which are issued by 500 large-cap companies traded on American stock exchanges (including the 30 companies that compose the Dow Jones Industrial Average). The index includes about 80 percent of the American market by capitalization. It is weighted by free-float market capitalization, so more valuable companies account for relatively more weight in the index. The index constituents and the constituent weights are updated regularly using rules published by S&P Dow Jones Indices. Although called the S&P 500, the index contains 503 stocks because it includes two share classes of stock from three of its component companies (Alphabet, Fox Corporation and News Corp).[1][2]

Selected changes to the list of S&P 500 components

[edit]

S&P Dow Jones Indices updates the components of the S&P 500 periodically, typically in response to acquisitions, or to keep the index up to date as various companies grow or shrink in value.[5] Between January 1, 1963, and December 31, 2014, 1,186 index components were replaced by other components.

Effective Date Added Removed Reason
TickerSecurityTickerSecurity
June 30, 2026CAGConagra BrandsMarket capitalization change.[6]
June 29, 2026HONAHoneywell AerospaceS&P 500 constituent Honeywell spun off Honeywell Aerospace.[6]
June 22, 2026 MRVL Marvell Technology POOL Pool Corporation Market capitalization change.[7]
June 22, 2026 FLEX Flex Ltd. CPB Campbell's Market capitalization change.[7]
June 2, 2026EPAMEPAM SystemsMarket capitalization change.[8]
June 1, 2026FDXFFedEx FreightS&P 500 & 100 constituent FedEx Corp. spun off FedEx Freight Holding.[8]
May 7, 2026 VEEV Veeva Systems CTRA Coterra Energy S&P 500 constituent Devon Energy Corp. acquired Coterra Energy. [9][10]
April 9, 2026CASYCasey'sHOLXHologicBlackstone Inc. and TPG Inc. acquired Hologic.[11]
March 23, 2026VRTVertivMTCHMatch GroupMarket capitalization change.[12]
March 23, 2026LITELumentumMOHMolina HealthcareMarket capitalization change.[12]
March 23, 2026COHRCoherent Corp.LWLamb WestonMarket capitalization change.[12]
March 23, 2026SATSEchoStarPAYCPaycomMarket capitalization change.[12]
February 9, 2026CIENCienaDAYDayforceThoma Bravo L.P. acquired Dayforce.[13]
December 22, 2025 CRH CRH LKQ LKQ Corporation Market capitalization change.[14]
December 22, 2025 CVNA Carvana SOLS Solstice Advanced Materials Market capitalization change.[14]
December 22, 2025 FIX Comfort Systems USA MHK Mohawk Industries Market capitalization change.[14]
December 11, 2025 ARES Ares Management K Kellanova Mars Inc. acquired Kellanova.[15]
November 28, 2025 SNDK Sandisk IPG Interpublic Group S&P 500 constituent Omnicom Group Inc. acquired (The) Interpublic Group.[16][17]
November 4, 2025 EMN Eastman Chemical Co. Market capitalization change.[18]
November 3, 2025 Q Qnity Electronics S&P 500 constituent Dupont de Nemours, Inc. spun off Qnity Electronics.[18]
October 31, 2025 KMX CarMax Market capitalization change.[18]
October 30, 2025 SOLS Solstice Advanced Materials S&P 500 and S&P 100 constituent Honeywell International Inc. spun off Solstice Advanced Materials.[18]
September 22, 2025 EME Emcor ENPH Enphase Energy Market capitalization change.[19]
September 22, 2025 HOOD Robinhood Markets CZR Caesars Entertainment Market capitalization change.[19]
